revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:兵庫縣姫路護國神社]] {{Referencesの明記|date=2017th year 8月28日 (月) 09:03 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hyōgo Prefecture Himeji Gokoku Shrine |image = [[File:Himejigokokujinja.JPG|260px|]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|50|8.6|N|134|41|40.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyogo|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, [[Honmachi, Himeji|Honmachi]] 118 |ISO = JP-28 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 50|緯度秒 = 8.6 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 40.6 |deity= War dead from the western region of Hyōgo Prefecture |神体 = |Rank= [[Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine|Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine]]
[[Beppyo Shrine]] |established= 昭和13rd year （{{ill|1938|en|1938|qid=Q18645|lt=1938|1=1938}}） |main hall = |alternative name = |札所等 = |festival= 5月2日
11月2日 |festival = |map = Japan Hyogo#Japan }} {{ill|Hyogo Himeji Gokoku Shrine|ja|兵庫縣姫路護國神社|1=Hyōgo Himeji Gokoku Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyogo Himeji Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyogo Himeji Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11393652}} is a {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} located in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, Japan. It enshrines 56,988 war dead from the western region of Hyōgo Prefecture (Banshu and Tajima districts). The shrine is situated within the middle bailey of {{ill|Himeji Castle|en|Himeji Castle|qid=Q188754|lt=Himeji Castle|1=Himeji Castle}} (currently {{ill|Himeji Park|ja|姫路公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji Park|1=Himeji Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11447463}}). == History == During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the area served as a samurai residence. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the middle bailey became a military training ground. Starting in 1893 (Meiji 26), annual {{ill|Shōkonsai|ja|招魂祭|1=Shōkonsai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsai|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsai|qq=}} (soul-inviting festivals) were held near the current site. In 1936 (Shōwa 11), the decision was made to build a formal shrine building, and in 1938 (Shōwa 13), the shrine was completed and enshrined. The following year, in 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Interior Minister. After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|qid=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, during the {{ill|Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|en|Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|qid=Q471889|lt=GHQ|1=Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers}} occupation, the shrine was renamed "Shirasagi-no-Miya" (White Heron Shrine) to avoid being seen as a militaristic facility. It reverted to its original name after Japan regained independence. The aging of bereaved families and comrades who have supported the shrine over the years, along with changing attitudes towards war memorials, have raised concerns about declining visitors. Hyōgo Prefecture also has another Gokoku Shrine, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture Kobe Gokoku Shrine|ja|兵庫縣神戸護國神社|1=Hyōgo Kobe Gokoku Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture Kobe Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyogo Kobe Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11393653}} in {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, which enshrines war dead from the eastern part of the prefecture. Near Himeji Castle, there is also the {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} {{ill|Itatehyōzu Shrine|en|Itatehyōzu Shrine|qid=Q11458547|lt=Shitatehiko Shrine|1=Itatehyōzu Shrine}} (Harima Province's Soja) and {{ill|Himeji Shrine|ja|姫路神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeji Shrine|1=Himeji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11447561}} which enshrines {{ill|Sakai Tadakatsu|ja|酒井正親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Tadakatsu|1=Sakai Masachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11644203}}. == Grounds == The shrine grounds include the main hall, administrative office, and a wedding hall and rest area called Shirasagi-no-Miya. Additionally, several memorials are found on the grounds, including: * "Tetsu 5454 Butai Hishima Sanka-hi" (Memorial for the scattered spirits of the 5454th Transport Regiment) * "Himeji Hohei Daihyaku-san-kyū Renpei Chinkon-hi" (Memorial for the spirits of the 139th Infantry Regiment) * "Dai-ju-nana Shidan Chinkon-hi" (Memorial for the 17th Division) == Gallery == {{Gallery | File:Himeji-castle and Himejigokokujinja.JPG|Himeji Castle and Himeji Gokoku Shrine | File:Himeji-gokoku-jinja 姫路護国神社・御祓いP7047455.JPG|Shrine building and purification ceremony}} == References == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/War memorial|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戦争記念施設|1=War memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q575759}} == External Links == * [http://www.himeji-gokoku.jp/index.html Hyōgo Prefecture Himeji Gokoku Shrine Official Website] * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_jQQfvitQgc Purification ceremony at Himeji Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|兵庫縣姫路護國神社|version=102008348|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:兵庫縣姫路護國神社]] {{Commons category|Hyogoken-Himeji-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11393652}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Himeji Castle]] [[Category:Hyogo Himeji Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
